refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) for operational use, ensuring data accuracy and availability for all relevant stakeholders.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly address timeliness, data accuracy, and availability to cover all aspects critical for operational use and safety.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of worksite model updates under representative operational scenarios; verify data accuracy via cross-check with ground truth; confirm availability through system uptime and interface responsiveness tests.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process sensor telemetry data within latency budget.
FR2: Update and maintain the worksite model data structure accurately and timely.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model to all relevant modules within latency constraints.

candidate_dps: DP1: Real-time telemetry ingestion and preprocessing pipeline.
DP2: Incremental worksite model update algorithm optimized for low latency.
DP3: Publish-subscribe distribution mechanism for worksite model updates.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of DP1, DP2, and DP3 to satisfy sub-FRs collectively.

potential_sources: Telemetry ingestion tightly coupled with model update algorithm.
Distribution mechanism depends on timely updates from model.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs form a coupled chain but maintain clear functional boundaries; modular design can mitigate diffusion.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link latency and reliability meet baseline requirements.
Risk of bottlenecks in telemetry processing under peak load.
Risk of update algorithm complexity causing latency spikes.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor latency performance closely and consider fallback or load shedding strategies.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry ingestion, model update, and distribution.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor data streams from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Distributes updated worksite model data to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes update logic and data flow, minimizing diffusion.
Contains telemetry sources but not update logic; would cause diffusion if assigned.
Primarily a consumer of updates; poor containment for update logic.
Receives alerts but does not process updates; unsuitable for containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update latency functionality within NGHE Solution module reduces interface complexity and latency risks.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure or performance bottleneck in NGHE Solution module.
Complexity of integrating telemetry ingestion, model update, and distribution in one module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider internal sub-modularization within NGHE Solution to isolate telemetry, update, and distribution functions.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs logically satisfy the refined sub-FRs with clear mapping and verification paths.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk by centralizing update logic in NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores strongly favor NGHE Solution, supporting architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed end-to-end via latency measurements and interface monitoring.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of update latency functionality within NGHE Solution module.

recommended_revisions: Refine internal modularization within NGHE Solution to manage complexity.
Define fallback strategies for latency spikes.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment are consistent, verifiable, and architecturally sound for this level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process sensor telemetry data within latency budget.
FR2: Update and maintain the worksite model data structure accurately and timely.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model to all relevant modules within latency constraints.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network link latency and reliability meet baseline requirements.
Telemetry data volume remains within processing capacity.
open_risks: Single point of failure in NGHE Solution module.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data under representative workloads.
Failure mode and effects analysis for NGHE Solution module.
